{"key_event":"Fiscal collapse and Shaba crises hollow Mobutu’s state","reasoning":"Mobutu’s MPR and security apparatus remained able to repress opposition and stage managed elections, but fiscal collapse, administrative decay, Shaba I/II, and reliance on foreign rescue pulled effective state capacity down into the weak-to-moderate range. Civil society remained heavily constrained by one-party rule, co-optation, and repression, with only modest gains from churches, informal networks, the Letter of the 13, and the founding of the UDPS.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":2.6,"state_change":-1.1,"state_power":4.7}
